danger_signals.visual_form: formations of enemy aircraft and ground installations moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: active combat threat; progression through level stages

danger_signals.visual_form: larger, distinct enemy unit at level conclusion
danger_signals.meaning: critical danger checkpoint; must be defeated to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: player craft reverts to base weapon set after hit
danger_signals.meaning: vulnerability state; temporary power reduction after damage

danger_signals.visual_form: stationary defensive structures on terrain below

safety_signals.meaning: weapon upgrade availability; temporary safety enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: designated structures to protect within level space
player repositioned slightly before death point

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: wave formations of distinct enemy aircraft types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scrolling environment revealing new enemy formations and space station positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: wave-based enemy spawning intensifies; boss appears at level conclusion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: weapon downgrade to base laser/missile when hit; craft appears in reduced power state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: reduced offensive capability until power-ups collected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemy aircraft formations visible on screen; ground defenses active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies move in coordinated patterns; projectiles fired at player; space stations under threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: power-up items appear in environment; visual weapon enhancement on collection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player craft gains upgraded weapons (stronger missiles, enhanced lasers)

environmental_storytelling.example: space stations positioned in vulnerable locations indicate where player must defend; their placement signals level difficulty zones
air-to-air enemies indicate aerial combat zone; ground installations below indicate need for missile weaponry—environmental design communicates tactical requirements
continuous upward scroll reveals new threats, establishing temporal progression and spatial scope of conflict across multiple defensive positions
larger, distinct enemy unit at level end visually signals mission culmination and highest danger point

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: fires enhanced missiles and lasers; improved offensive capability visible in weapon projectile patterns
reverts to base weapon loadout; slower/weaker fire pattern; repositioned to earlier level point
moves in coordinated wave formations; fires projectiles at player; advances toward space stations
stationary; fires at approaching player; requires ground-targeted weaponry to destroy
distinct movement pattern; sustained offensive fire; requires multiple hits to defeat; signals level completion upon destruction

notes: Alpha Mission (ASO: Armored Scrum Object in Japan) is a vertically scrolling shooter with clear air-to-air/air-to-ground weapon segregation. Visual communication relies heavily on enemy type differentiation (aerial vs. ground-based), power-up visibility, and boss distinctiveness. The game uses spatial positioning and wave patterns to communicate threat escalation. Limited documentary evidence exists on specific color coding or faction symbology; communication appears primarily through entity type, positioning, and behavioral manifestation rather than faction coloration.
